biography.value:
The Lonelywood Banshee is the spectral remnant of a female elf warrior, banished from her people for a selfish and evil act. Condemned to eternal exile in the Lonelywood forest, she manifests as a wailing spirit wielding a spectral longbow that fires phantom arrows.

There is only one banshee haunting the forest. If destroyed, she reforms after 24 hours in a random location within the Lonelywood, damned to her eternal exile.

details.source: Icewind Dale: Rime of the Frostmaiden

description.value: The banshee can magically sense the presence of living creatures up to 5 miles away. She knows the general direction they're in but not their exact locations.
The banshee can move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. She takes 5 (1d10) force damage if she ends her turn inside an object.
Melee Spell Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) necrotic damage.
Ranged Spell Attack: +4 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (3d6 + 2) necrotic damage. This phantom bow functions like the banshee's Corrupting Touch but is made as a ranged attack.

Each non-undead creature within 60 feet of the banshee that can see her must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened for 1 minute. A frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. If a target's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, the target is immune to the banshee's Horrifying Visage for the next 24 hours.

items.name: Wail (Recharges after a Short or Long Rest)

description.value: The banshee releases a mournful wail, provided that she isn't in sunlight. This wail has no effect on constructs and undead. All other creatures within 30 feet of her that can hear her must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature drops to 0 hit points. On a success, a creature takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage.
